# criblio_global_var (Resource)

GlobalVar Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_global_var" "my_global_var" {
  args = [
    {
      type = "number"
      name = "rate"
    }
  ]
  description = "Global Variable that computes a dynamic threshold based on the specified args"
  group_id = "default"
  id = "dynamic_threshold"
  lib = "custom"
  type = "expression"
  value = "rate * 1.5"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tools/codegen -->
## Schema

### Required

- `group_id` (String) Worker group ID.
- `id` (String) Global variable name
- `type` (String)

### Optional

- `args` (Attributes List) Argument definitions for expression-type variables. Each item has type and name (e.g. for (val / 1073741824).toFixed(precision || 5)). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--args))
- `description` (String)
- `lib` (String)
- `tags` (String)
- `value` (String)

<a id="nestedatt--args"></a>
### Nested Schema for `args`

Required:

- `type` (String) Argument type (e.g. number, string)
- `name` (String) Argument name

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_global_var.my_criblio_global_var
  id = jsonencode({
    group_id = "default"
    id       = "ENV"
  })
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_global_var.my_criblio_global_var '{"group_id": "default", "id": "ENV"}'
```
